place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Admiralty MRT (NS10) is an elevated station on Singapore's North-South Line (NSL) in the heart of Woodlands, known for its seamless integration with Kampung Admiralty, an innovative senior-focused development featuring housing, a medical centre, and a hawker centre. It serves as a vital transport hub connecting residents via MRT and bus to the city and other parts of Singapore, offering amenities like shops and food options within its premises and nearby Causeway Point, reflecting the area's transition from a former naval base to a modern, family-friendly hub.
Key Features:
Line: North-South Line (NSL).
Operator: SMRT Corporation.
Structure: Elevated, with two tracks and two platforms.
Connectivity: Excellent links to Woodlands, Yishun, and the Central area; near expressways (SLE, BKE).
Accessibility: Wheelchair accessible with bicycle facilities.
Notable Integrations:
Kampung Admiralty: Directly linked, this unique vertical village combines public housing for seniors with a medical centre, childcare, and a hawker centre.
Causeway Point: A major shopping mall adjacent to the station, offering extensive retail and dining.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of the older stations in NSL. Simple, straightforward, elevator and escalator accesses, and connect directly to Admiralty Place. Not exactly a mall, but a consolidation of many facilities like wet market, supermarkets, eateries, coffee places, pharmacies, doctors, money changer, fast food.

However, while there are many atm machines here, there is no bank here. There used to be an UOB Bank but it has been replaced.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Admiralty mrt is now a very convenient place with lots of amenities and food. Just in front of the mrt station is the  Admiralty Place mall and Kampong Admiralty hawker centre. Within the vicinity, you can find 3 supermarkets - 24hr Fairprice, Prime and Giant and also a wet market.
You can also find a Cantine food court, KFC, Subway, A&W and a large 2 storey McDonald's here. Overall, this makes Admiralty a much more pleasant and convenient place for the residents living here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops

Freshly prepared healthy ingridients with tender loving caring skillful cooking and professional warm services.  Shopping includes shops in the overhead bridge POSB banking Kumpang Admiralty Kampung  Admiralty hawker centre Admiralty place Giant supermarkets entertainment and amenities including wet market and NTUC foodfair and fair price. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats locals visitors and families.

Ample parking, and affiliate prices if you're a SAFRA/CSC/Passion card member.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A good bowl of Lormee should have that thick, gooey yet smooth gravy and packed with flavour. Unfortunately, this Lormee at Yishun Park (早碗见面 Lor mee & Satay Beehoon) , gravy was diluted and has no umami taste.

Even after adding vinegar, garlic and chilli, it’s still pretty one dimensional. The only saving grace was their fried fish, nicely fried, soft and still has that slight crunchy exterior.

Damage: $4.80 (small bowl)

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Family went there for lunch on a Saturday at around 1130H. It was not too crowded, and we managed to find a table without needing to wait. Parking was okay too.

The hawker centre was big and airy, we didn't feel hot. The tables were not so clean, and no one seemed to be going around to wipe the tables too.

We ordered (1) fish sliced soup, (2) Ming jian Kueh, (3) kids nasi lemak meal, (4) ice kachang.

Food is pretty much the standard stuff... just two things to say - (1) Shrinkflation is real - could have more fish slices in the soup, and (2) nasi lemak rice was fragrant but the chicken fillet was dry and tasteless.

Overall, still prefer Canberra hawker centre for it's range and quality of food!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I would like to introduce a delicious Pao Fan at Stall 01-35A if you are dining at Yishun Park Market, 51 Yishun Ave 11.

Look for Chef Kelvin — he is very polite and courteous. The fried fish is freshly fried, and every grain of rice is fragrant and perfectly cooked.

Highly recommended if you are in the area!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Honestly, this is the best food I've ever tasted. You won't find this kind of food anywhere else but in Türkiye or Lebanon.I recommend everyone try this new and sophisticated food with delicious flavors.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I stopped by for breakfast after spotting their Bubur Kampung, which looked too good to pass up. It didn't disappoint the flavors were delicious and the pricing was spot on. The place has a great atmosphere and is kept very clean. I’ll definitely be back!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I stopped by for breakfast after spotting their Bubur Kampung, which looked too good to pass up. It didn't disappoint the flavors were delicious and the pricing was spot on. The place has a great atmosphere and is kept very clean. I’ll definitely be back!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A good bowl of Lormee should have that thick, gooey yet smooth gravy and packed with flavour. Unfortunately, this Lormee at Yishun Park (早碗见面 Lor mee & Satay Beehoon) , gravy was diluted and has no umami taste.

Even after adding vinegar, garlic and chilli, it’s still pretty one dimensional. The only saving grace was their fried fish, nicely fried, soft and still has that slight crunchy exterior.

Damage: $4.80 (small bowl)

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Family went there for lunch on a Saturday at around 1130H. It was not too crowded, and we managed to find a table without needing to wait. Parking was okay too.

The hawker centre was big and airy, we didn't feel hot. The tables were not so clean, and no one seemed to be going around to wipe the tables too.

We ordered (1) fish sliced soup, (2) Ming jian Kueh, (3) kids nasi lemak meal, (4) ice kachang.

Food is pretty much the standard stuff... just two things to say - (1) Shrinkflation is real - could have more fish slices in the soup, and (2) nasi lemak rice was fragrant but the chicken fillet was dry and tasteless.

Overall, still prefer Canberra hawker centre for it's range and quality of food!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I would like to introduce a delicious Pao Fan at Stall 01-35A if you are dining at Yishun Park Market, 51 Yishun Ave 11.

Look for Chef Kelvin — he is very polite and courteous. The fried fish is freshly fried, and every grain of rice is fragrant and perfectly cooked.

Highly recommended if you are in the area!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Located just outside Sembawang MRT, Sun Plaza is a heartland mall that has plenty of things that one might need. Sometimes I am amazed about how many shops it can fit inside.

There is a food court, multiple shops including a shop with eateries inside (Scarlett), big supermarket and multiple pharmacies in the basement, and there is some kind of balcony where we watched the Dragon Dance (it’s CNY when we visited). There are ATMs as well, sport shop like Decathlon (not as big as Decathlon experience but slightly bigger than their other small branches). There is money changer, multiple coffee shops, household necessity, quick hair-cut, pet shop (with surprisingly good range of products despite the size), stationery/book shop (which has become a rarity here), even computer and handphone accessories.

I would say it’s better to come here instead of Causeway Point (Woodland MRT) because it’s less crowded, cuter (I think the mall has its charm) and there are a very good range of shops and merchandizes.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A mall invested with Big House Flies. From when you're dinning (Wang Cafe) to literally just standing outside Hugabo while waiting for your family in the toilet, the pesty houseflies just keep hovering. With so many F&B establishments I do not understand how this can be hygienic. Thankfully this ain't my neighbourhood mall

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Sun Plaza holds a lot of good memories for me, as I used to visit this mall very frequently between 2017 and 2019. It has always been a fantastic and highly convenient community hub, perfectly situated right next to the Sembawang MRT station. Even back then, I loved how it offered a great mix of retail and dining—from the famous White Restaurant to the always-reliable Haidilao. It’s a well-organized and compact mall that is very easy to navigate, whether you're there for a quick grocery run or a long meal with friends. Seeing how it has remained a vibrant and essential part of the North for so many years is wonderful. It truly caters to the daily needs of the community with its library, supermarket, and diverse shops. A solid 5-star rating for a place that has consistently provided great experiences over the years!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: As I live in the vicinity, I get to visit it very often. A relatively small mall, but fully self-sufficient. Has the usual spread of tenant shops like NTUC, ToastBox,  StarBucks, KouFu etc.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mall with lots of food and amenities.

Down side the lift are limited and you need to queue. Carpark space are kinda tight and also limited during peak hours.

Good part: many food options available and layout of mall it’s quite compact on the lower levels

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It is a park located in sembangwang. It is near the coast and used to be a navy base. So there are things inspired by the naval heritage like the compass and the battleship playground here. It is only accesible by bus 882 and it come in 15 min frequency. Not much people here but can catch a glismp of malaysia.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Nice park located at the coast off Sembawang. Quiet enough for a good walk and chat with a friend. It has a nice and clean toilet in the middle of the park. There's also an activity area for the kids and young people. There's a jetty for casual fishing. You can also see parts of Malaysia in the distance..

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Nice quiet park and great for taking slow walk. BBQ pits available for booking. Nice shelter and a beautiful playground. There is a Western and seafood restaurant. Dog lovers will be happy to bring their pet dogs here for a run... Plenty of plants and trees. Even passed by a place where plants lovers could rent a small plot of "land" to start farming 😅

There is bus serving this area too!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 5-Star Review – Sembawang Beach

If you are looking for a peaceful and nostalgic seaside experience in Singapore, Sembawang Beach is truly a hidden gem. 🌅🏝️

The moment you arrive, you are welcomed by the calming sound of waves, soft golden sand, and the refreshing sea breeze. Unlike the more crowded beaches in Singapore, this place feels wonderfully relaxed and natural. It’s the kind of beach where you can slow down and enjoy the simple beauty of sun, sand, and sea. ☀️🌊

One of the most heartwarming sights here is families and friends gathering along the shoreline with small nets and buckets. Children laugh excitedly while trying to catch tiny crabs and small fish in the shallow water. Watching everyone work together—some holding nets, others spotting crabs hiding under rocks—creates a joyful and nostalgic kampung-style atmosphere. 🦀🐟

The beach is also perfect for:
•	Picnics with family and friends under the trees 🌳
•	Relaxing sunset walks along the quiet coastline 🚶‍♂️
•	Fishing or crabbing during low tide 🎣
•	Enjoying a peaceful break away from the busy city life

The nearby park area is clean, well maintained, and has plenty of space for children to run around safely. When the sun begins to set, the sky turns beautiful shades of orange and pink, making the whole beach feel magical. 🌇

Overall, Sembawang Beach offers something increasingly rare in Singapore — a simple, authentic seaside experience where nature, family, and friendship come together. A perfect place to relax, reconnect, and create happy memories. ❤️

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A large park not far from the Republic Polytechnic and the upcoming new RTS terminus of Woodlands North. It's also the gateway to Woodlands Waterfront Jetty and its seafood restaurant. The premise encompasses the large playground with multiple slides, some as high as the ridge surrounding the playground itself, and the mangrove forest.

An interesting fact I found during my visit - the playground has the rare swing for wheelchair users, although this was inoperable in time being.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Came here 7 years ago when it was new, and the place was nice and new. Kids had a lot of fun there. 7 years later, the place is so fun down, and most of the slides are closed for repairs. There's also green moss on every surface. Facilities were no better, the toilet is not very clean and everything was just not well maintained. Really night and day. Hope the repairs bring it back to its former glory.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A decent-sized park in the north, around Woodland neighborhood. What I like about this park is the feel. Going inside into it, it takes me out of the urban space where I can usually enjoy the nature without feeling that I am in the middle of the city.

There are multiple entrances into this park. For those who wants to take bus, there is a direct bus from Woodlands interchange where one can alight near Woodland waterfront, if I remember correctly. We used to come here quite often for macro shots, taking pictures of insects, hunching our backs over some shrubs.

For those who enjoy walking, this can be a decent walking/jogging route. Some parts are quite flat, while near the end where the mangrove is more hilly with some ups and downs and even the ramp type road on a spot (picture).

When I came here again (March 2026) it still feels nice, but some maintenance is needed. Missing and rusty signages, stagnant water, some trash (this has to be attributed to irresponsible visitors), but other than that, it still looks like what I remember, except for some ongoing maintenances and constructions around the park.

There was a sign of Bridge C closing (near the mangrove), but it was small and located in the middle of a shelter that I didn’t stop by because I didn’t need to take a rest. As a result, I missed the announcement and walked all the way to the Mangrove Forest from the wrong trail. I ended up in front of the hoarded up area and had to circle back through another trail until I found the mangrove and the other side of the hoarding.

I saw a bunch of monkeys, a bunch of otters, a big monitor lizards, and since my main purpose is to walk, I didn’t go for macro hunt. There is a big playground in the park, a water lily pond, mangrove forest, and nice natural trails. Overall, I like this park because it still feels natural and it’s not that crowded or popular so I can still enjoy a nice walks. During weekdays, there are older folks. The monkeys are respectful, leave them alone and they won’t bother you. Not sure if this are different groups or the ones we usually saw back then.

There are playgrounds, ampitheatre, mangrove inside here.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Beautiful nature and a great route for jogging! Wish the park could’ve been more preserved as you can tell that it’s been awhile since someone came to
do any cleaning. Citizen needs to clear their own trash as it can be seen everywhere which will definitely affect the wildlife there.

Would be great if we can install lightings for night walk/run as it can get pitch black once the sun set.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This park offers a wonderful setting for a family outing. Children can delight in the available slides and swings. There is also ample space provided for enjoyable cycling and jogging. It is a truly pleasant environment for outdoor activities.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Modern and open space hawker centre with high ceilings and big as fan (sorry might get banned for spelling the real name). The food here is affordable but taste wise, very plain and basic, catered to the old people. The Bak Kut teh that food king reviewed is over hyped (see my review for this). Pleasantly surprised by the ah Ma meesua when I gave it a second chance. It tastes better when dining in than take away. Avoid egg tarts at all costs as it tastes like rock. Friend say it’s usually good but when he bought at 8pm, the taste is bad.

One downside is the seats. Very hard to find proper seats especially during peak hours as the seats are all weirdly shaped and built for 8-10 people. The designer of this place doesn’t know that loners with no family or friends exist in the society. Even for family of 2, also need to share table. I guess, if you like to make friends with strangers sitting at the same table, do give here a visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This hawker centre offers a good variety of traditional local food at reasonable prices, making it a pleasant spot for everyday meals.
For vegans and vegetarians, there are several welcoming options. Gong Fu Yuan Vegetarian serves a range of Chinese vegetarian dishes with many vegan-friendly choices, while Xi De Li offers classic fried vegan snacks that are both nostalgic and satisfying.

Even non-vegetarian stalls provide meatless options. Kopi Kiosk includes vegetarian selections within its Signature Breakfast Sets, Hao Cheng Kee Carrot Cake offers a meat-free carrot cake that’s flavourful and comforting, and Saamsudeen Indian Muslim Stall has various Prata options suitable for those avoiding meat.

Overall, it’s a convenient and enjoyable place to find tasty local food, with plenty of choices for plant-based diners.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
It’s only my third visit here after so many years, and I’m surprised by how popular the Muslim food stalls are at Kampung Admiralty Hawker Centre—even on a weekday dinner hour. The queues were so long I almost fainted just looking at them!

I ended up choosing Soon Kee Roasted Delights. The roast pork skin was crispy and flavourful—loved the crunch. The lower layer had quite a bit of thick, soft fat, which fans of that texture would definitely enjoy. I even added some chicken rice chilli to my plain white rice (I know, unorthodox), and it turned out surprisingly tasty. 🤤

Although the space is large and not too crowded, I still had a hard time finding a seat. 😅

But hey, taste and service are always subjective. ☺️

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Eatbook article was in 2017, so when we finally checked this place out, some stores were no longer around.

We often visit Causeway Point and even Bukit Canberra HC and Marsiling HC than this place.

I often read that Kampung Admiralty is an elderly care housing estate.

This HC is run by Kopitiam but they don't run the 10% discount for Fairprice app and Merdeka users. The price is somewhat kept low.

I suppose we won't find it as appealing if we do stay around this precinct.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
As a local Singaporean, I was pleasantly surprised to find numerous food stalls offering delicious meals for just $3! Contrary to the belief that affordable dining is hard to come by in our city, these budget-friendly options are not only tasty but also showcase our vibrant food culture.

For anyone looking to enjoy great meals without spending a fortune, I highly recommend exploring these $3 stalls. It's an excellent way to experience the flavors of Singapore!

However, unlike most stations on the NSL, the two platforms to board the train are separate, so keep an eye out for which direction you're heading before going up to the platform level. There's also a direct link to the Canberra Plaza.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Did not personally visit the gym but wanted to give my sincerest thanks to their wonderful staff Adelin and Fariz (apologies if I misspelled your names), who helped my father during a bad fall outside the gym premise. Both staff members took prompt action upon seeing his fall and immediately brought a first aid kit and oxygen mask, reassuring my father while we were waiting for the ambulance. Thank you again 🙏

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Big field with alot of activities that I can do there, the gym is usually packed and it is right beside a swimming pool so I can cool off after a gym session. However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I went to the library 10 minutes before its closing time to print a document. Thought it would be straightforward so 10 minutes would be enough. Turns out it’s more complicated than I expected (need to switch to some internet browser mode and wait 1min which I wasn’t aware of). One of the staff on duty, nametag Yeen Mei, was patient in guiding me on how to print despite it being so near closing time. In the end we managed to print the document before 10min was up. Thank you Yeen Mei !!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice Public Library. I like how the New Arrivals and Recently Returned Books are just a shelf near the entrance. The library is quite huge with small areas for you to study. There are also charging ports at the study desks if you need to charge your phone/laptops. It is very noisy at night when the library is about to close as a lot of kids do come here. I don't really recommend studying here, but the selection of books is quite a lot. Wide array of magazines especially the National Geographic ones

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: First time visiting this Sembawang public library. Very cosy venue on first impression, lighting not overly bright but not too dim to make users to fall asleep. I noticed this venue got ramp, i think is for baby pram easy access. Even got a few decks for students to use and stretch the legs, thats absolute lovely. Love the overall design. Check out the interior pictures too. Worth to visit for library hopping. :p

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I understand that library should be quiet and conducive for everyone. Hence seeing all the negative comments saying the children are loud in the library i understand why librarian keep reminding to lower the volume.

However since it is a children section, children are expected to be children. My child grabbed a book, librarian immediately said "are you borrowing it?" for every book my child took. This scared her that she didn't want to browse any books anymore. Then when we were reading in the children section, they just kept coming and say lower our volume, when we weren't even loud, my child wasn't making any noise. 3 librarians just kept hovering over us the entire time there that make it so unenjoyable so we decided to leave.

The built of the children section had this grassy patch them children can sit on. But it's hollow so whenever someone walks over it, its make a "thump" sound, even for a child who is much lighter. Hence i gets why it because noisy for others. I think instead of blaming children for being noisy the library can think of ways to make it less noisy or just enclose up the children section. It is literally a children section while children can be children.

While we leaving we witness another set of parents asked by the librarian to ask theor child not to grab a book. I guess we are not the only one experiencing it. Would rather go other libraries with children section if this continues. It really takes the curiosity of learning and reading out of children when they can't even get a book out of the shelves.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I switched from Yishun Library and have been visiting Sembawang Library for the past few months as it is closer to my home.

On a few occasions, I searched for a book using the app and located the shelf, but was unable to find it. When I approached staff for assistance, they were also unable to locate the book and mentioned it was likely misplaced. This happened more than once.

However, on another visit, I met a staff member, Ms Ong Cheng Luan Agnes, who was very proactive. She took down the book details, followed up, and eventually managed to locate it. I really appreciate her initiative and helpfulness.

In the meantime, I had already made a reservation and incurred the reservation fee. Thankfully, I was still able to get the book in the end.

Feedback: It would be great if other staff could be more proactive and supportive in assisting patrons.

Shoutout: A big thank you to *Ms Agnes* for going the extra mile to help!

There are also charging ports at the study desks if you need to charge your phone/laptops. It is very noisy at night when the library is about to close as a lot of kids do come here. I don't really recommend studying here, but the selection of books is quite a lot. Wide array of magazines especially the National Geographic ones

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: First time visiting this Sembawang public library. Very cosy venue on first impression, lighting not overly bright but not too dim to make users to fall asleep. I noticed this venue got ramp, i think is for baby pram easy access. Even got a few decks for students to use and stretch the legs, thats absolute lovely. Love the overall design. Check out the interior pictures too. Worth to visit for library hopping. :p

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I understand that library should be quiet and conducive for everyone. Hence seeing all the negative comments saying the children are loud in the library i understand why librarian keep reminding to lower the volume.

However since it is a children section, children are expected to be children. My child grabbed a book, librarian immediately said "are you borrowing it?" for every book my child took. This scared her that she didn't want to browse any books anymore. Then when we were reading in the children section, they just kept coming and say lower our volume, when we weren't even loud, my child wasn't making any noise. 3 librarians just kept hovering over us the entire time there that make it so unenjoyable so we decided to leave.

The built of the children section had this grassy patch them children can sit on. But it's hollow so whenever someone walks over it, its make a "thump" sound, even for a child who is much lighter. Hence i gets why it because noisy for others. I think instead of blaming children for being noisy the library can think of ways to make it less noisy or just enclose up the children section. It is literally a children section while children can be children.

While we leaving we witness another set of parents asked by the librarian to ask theor child not to grab a book. I guess we are not the only one experiencing it. Would rather go other libraries with children section if this continues. It really takes the curiosity of learning and reading out of children when they can't even get a book out of the shelves.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I switched from Yishun Library and have been visiting Sembawang Library for the past few months as it is closer to my home.

On a few occasions, I searched for a book using the app and located the shelf, but was unable to find it. When I approached staff for assistance, they were also unable to locate the book and mentioned it was likely misplaced. This happened more than once.

However, on another visit, I met a staff member, Ms Ong Cheng Luan Agnes, who was very proactive. She took down the book details, followed up, and eventually managed to locate it. I really appreciate her initiative and helpfulness.

In the meantime, I had already made a reservation and incurred the reservation fee. Thankfully, I was still able to get the book in the end.

Feedback: It would be great if other staff could be more proactive and supportive in assisting patrons.

Shoutout: A big thank you to *Ms Agnes* for going the extra mile to help!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I went to the library 10 minutes before its closing time to print a document. Thought it would be straightforward so 10 minutes would be enough. Turns out it’s more complicated than I expected (need to switch to some internet browser mode and wait 1min which I wasn’t aware of). One of the staff on duty, nametag Yeen Mei, was patient in guiding me on how to print despite it being so near closing time. In the end we managed to print the document before 10min was up. Thank you Yeen Mei !!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Sembawang MRT station connects people to many facilities and amenities. It links to Canberra Hill, a home to important service like polyclinic, pharmacy, Active SG swimming pool and gym, various sport halls and events like zumba, yoga etc. There is a big food court there and a dog run (tbh, I haven't seen what the dog rum looks like, but unfortunately the food court is not pet friendly despite the big space and being outdoor)).

Outside the MRT is Sun Plaza, a decent heartland mall and there is a free shuttle bus to SSC (Sembawang Shopping Center) a better and humble shopping center (in my opinion).

There is a Kopitiam in the MRT as well and cafe. The Kopitiam's choices are not that great and the prices are higher, but it’s strategic for commuter. Other shops include Cheers, salon, dental, atms.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Sembawang MRT Station 🚉 is conveniently located next to Sun Plaza . There are 24 hours convenience store Cheers , Kopitiam food court, bakery , pawn shop, coffeeshop, food establishments inside the MRT Station 🚉  … provides a lot of convenience to people who just got off the train 🚆 and need to quickly grab something to eat or buy some essentials.

The MRT Station 🚉 control staff are always professional and courteous .

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I sold my house at CCK and bought at Sembawang. Price much cheaper for past 5 yrs. Sembawang is quiet and less commuters. Not so busy and easy to walk around. 24hrs of Chinese, Muslim and western food along semb Dr @ 463. End 2029 semb port will be Converted to new town and sport activities.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
My email to SMRT today..

Dear Customer Relations Team,

I am writing to raise a concern regarding an incident reported to us by my brother that occurred on 21 December at approximately 2.10–2.15 pm at Sembawang MRT station.

According to my brother, he observed an elderly man using his mobile phone to zoom in on and take photographs of two females who were standing near the station map area. The conduct appeared targeted rather than incidental.

My brother informed one of the station staff members at the baggage check area on what happened, and suggested that they look into the matter. However, he was told that because the incident occurred in a public area, it was acceptable, unless the individual was pointing the phone at a specific person. My brother again clarified that the man had indeed zoomed in and directed his phone towards the two females.

I am extremely concerned both about the incident itself and the response given by the staff member. While photography in public spaces may not always be unlawful, targeted or intrusive photography particularly when zooming in on individuals warrant intervention or at least monitoring.

I would appreciate it if the station could:

1. Review the incident, including any available CCTV footage from the relevant time and location; and

2. Reinforce guidance to frontline staff on how to respond appropriately when members of the public report potentially intrusive or concerning behavior.

The intention of this email is to ensure commuter/public safety and to prevent similar or more serious incidents from occurring in the future.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: (Maxwell Chicken rice.) The food was absolutely delicious. Service was great. The soup for some reason tastes like cucumber. Ordering Kiosks give discounts(why?), but there were a lot of people. (Sugarcane from Scissors Paper Stone) The sugarcane tastes awesome, sweet and cooling. It costs $2.00. The time taken for it to be served was really quick.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Modern food court in a precinct mall with plenty of facilities nearby.

Hawker centre is open air with lots of space in between. Plenty to choose from. Have pre order kiosks

Coolest thing here is that there are family tables that a slightly lower than the standard height so kids can eat without booster seats!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Brunch with the Wife here today. So good food to choose from. Local as well as Middle Eastern and Western.  Michelin accredited stalls as well and relatively inexpensive.  Had the Teochew fishball noodles today as its handmade , originally from Kim Keat in the 70s. Oh so good 👍.  Give it a try the noodles and the place.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Superb hawker centre. It serves some of the best local cuisine to Thai Food one can ask for.

The must try(s) are so many. Ranging from
Li Xin’s fish cake to Pink’s mango sticky rice to Pork Boat Noodle. The XLB and Dumplings are a must try too. So is the Muah Chee.

The hawker is clean, spacious, and well ventilated. Superb place. Strongly recommend this place to the foodie.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A well designed Hawker Centre with numbers to identify the different blocks and wayfinding. I tried the fried carrot cake, it was nice. The price considered reasonable in 2026, with the portion ok.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text:
I visited Playfacto Woods Square last year and met Ms Celine and Ms Wind. I love how cosy the centre feels and how amicable the ladies were.

I placed my boy in for adhoc care. On his first day, the teacher fetches him from the lobby and brought him to the centre safely. During the day, they also updated me how my boy was coping.

When I picked my boy, he shared that he like the teacher Ms Lee Lee and the student care very much! And he finds the food delicious too!
(I guess better than mummy cook 🤣)

As a parent, I feel assured that my child is in good hands and that he receives kind attention and care from the teachers. Thank you to all the staff for your heart ❤️ work in ensuring the children have the most comforting and nurturing environment after school. 👍👍👍

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
I would highly recommend Star Learners Sembawang Way to other parents. The teachers are friendly, caring and approachable, which gives me peace of mind. Since joining the school, I have seen positive changes in my child. He has become more independent and is now able to eat on his own. His speech has also improved, and he is using many more words to express himself.

The school provides a variety of engaging indoor activities that support the children's learning and development. Beyond the classroom, they also organise regular excursions that allow the children to explore new environments and gain meaningful experiences. What makes these excursions even more special is that one parent is allowed to join, creating precious opportunities for parent-child bonding while sharing in the child's learning journey.

Another highlight is the additional enrichment programmes, such as Zumbini, which make learning fun and engaging through music, movement and interaction.

I am grateful for the support and dedication of the teachers and would definitely recommend Star Learners to other parents.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
We are extremely happy with our son’s experience at Star Learners @ Sembawang Way. Principal Ms Su provides excellent leadership and ensures a warm, welcoming, and well-organised environment for both children and parents.

The teachers are caring, patient, and dedicated. A special thank you to the N1 teachers for their support and commitment. My son looks forward to attending school each day, which speaks volumes about the positive environment.

We have seen great progress in our son, not only academically but also in his confidence and social skills.

The centre management is also very efficient, and communication with parents is always clear and timely. We truly appreciate the effort and commitment of the entire team and would highly recommend this centre to other parents!

review_text:
My son has been with Star Learners since the beginning of this year.

I have heard good things about Star Learners but I fully accept the fact that their school and their faculty is amazing.

Ever since my son enrolled with them, he has gained confidence, improved in his academics and is able to express himself even better.

The teachers are extremely friendly, caring and most importantly, patient with him. They ensure they maintain a good rapport with the parents and also ensure the parents feel safe and secure for sending their children to school.

I am glad that my son is enrolled here and I hope he grows even more confident, happy and pleased in the coming years.

Special thanks to the Principal of this Center, Ms Su and Huang Laoshi whom are very engaging with the kids, my son and for being very supportive.

place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My son starts N1 in early Jan 2026 and distance is my first priority so i chosen this branch.
My son took bout 1 mth before dropping all the crying drama and detour from the centre.
The teachers know how to handle ithe cry baby  and i dont have to worry once he enter the centre.
The curiculum part wise is vy good and my son show  alot of skills like vy agile in handling artworks and glue (before that scare of glue cox will dirty his hand). He also will sing many songs tht teacher taught. He also start feeding himself independently.
Language improves tremendously too.
And kudos to the mgt staff too who help me in the subsidy resubmission which i thought i is going couldn't get it.
I love every staff there as they really remember every child name, comforting & talk to them.
. They make sure every child go into each class & fully attended.
My son class teacher, Teacher Rina and Teacher Huang will try update  me as much possible if i see them be it whether he sleep or drink milk, etc. After 1 mth, my son already adapted to the routine which is important. Now, whenever i go and pick my son he is reluctant to go back as he want to play with friends there.
Lately they hold a workshop with parenta on kids emotion control and is helpful to help parent to understand that each child brain is still developing on emotion control.
Safety and hygiene is also strictly implemented. each child is checked thoroughly before they arr allowed to enter.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
One of the best preschools in Sembawang! You will never regret enrolling your child here. The support staff and teachers are consistently warm, friendly, and genuinely caring. Every morning during health checks, I notice how they greet each child with smiles and encouragement — such a simple gesture, but it shows how much they value each little one.

The Centre Principal, Ms Su, leads with so much heart and passion. She is always approachable, full of positivity, and quick to respond to our queries. Whenever feedback is shared, she receives it graciously and takes immediate steps to address it, which gives us great confidence as parents.

The learning environment is not only nurturing but also engaging — you can see how the children are happy, growing, and thriving. As parents, we feel reassured knowing that our child is in a place where teachers care deeply, leadership is strong, and communication is open.

All in all, we are truly thankful to have come across this preschool.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
I would highly recommend Star Learners Sembawang Way to other parents. The teachers are friendly, caring and approachable, which gives me peace of mind. Since joining the school, I have seen positive changes in my child. He has become more independent and is now able to eat on his own. His speech has also improved, and he is using many more words to express himself.

The school provides a variety of engaging indoor activities that support the children's learning and development. Beyond the classroom, they also organise regular excursions that allow the children to explore new environments and gain meaningful experiences. What makes these excursions even more special is that one parent is allowed to join, creating precious opportunities for parent-child bonding while sharing in the child's learning journey.

Another highlight is the additional enrichment programmes, such as Zumbini, which make learning fun and engaging through music, movement and interaction.

I am grateful for the support and dedication of the teachers and would definitely recommend Star Learners to other parents.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
We are extremely happy with our son’s experience at Star Learners @ Sembawang Way. Principal Ms Su provides excellent leadership and ensures a warm, welcoming, and well-organised environment for both children and parents.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Am I the only one feeling a bit hot in here? 🤔 Haven't come to this library and the cafe downstairs for a long time. I prefer here because many available seats. And I am someone who appreciates some little privacy so I can say it is better (than West Mall 😅) although we can't control the noise of some people. Hope they don't chit chat in the library...

Hope they can volume down a bit too. Even the Level 4 kids section was moderate. Convenient too, there is toilet. That's how I found out my menses comes rather sudden and unexpectedly 😭

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Other libary blast the air con which is okay
This libary doesn’t bother to turn it on on the upper floors??? Booked S301 and when I came there was a pile of stuff there, told the librarian but she said cannot do anything.. the person never came back and I had to book another seat which this uncle was using my wall socket: asked staff again tell me don’t be so annoying then I put one of my power bank at the quickcharge station, staff told me cannot charge power banks?? It literally says you can charge power banks?

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Convenient place to study. Relaxing and clean. The staff provided enough tables and chairs. But the only things is very limited socket to charge your laptop. It is better to fully charge your devices before come :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Excellent place for self study at level 3. Level have cafe and sofa chair. And also charging station for phone powerbank and ipad etc. There is also workshop for enhancement on IT product. But might be different every month.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Don’t let the entrance deceive you. From the outside it may look a small and simple library. The lib actually do have 4 stories, and each story has a vast collection of books. Unlike other libraries, Woodland library may have the largest space all with comfy sofa or study desk. It also have a cafe if you need a quick coffee fix. Kids area upstairs.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimmimg complex has 1 kids' water play area and a 50m length pool. Not a big complex but sufficient for the number of users.

There is a big pond when looking out from pool, open space and doesn't feel clamp. There is a haeker center across the street and a LRT station.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: 1st time been there to join SG Digital course.  It's has good environment facing a pond n a swimming pool.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Nice environment outside with fish pond, but 1 of the worst inside. Too many swimming schools using the lanes and left with 1/3 of the pool for the public. Everything in SG runs like a business. The kid's pool is really small. Overall, the swimming pools is not suitable for exercise.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Damn U guys better get Ur info correct. So many directions given, no officers that have good knowledge, I'm going around in circles. Why can't U guys just say if it's for appeals to join the queue rather than making a call that waste my time and energy. Employ officers that have more knowledge and information! I wish I can give 0 stars if I could.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Beautiful community club next to a pond. Comes with many features like dance studios, badminton court, swimming pool, barber, food centre, etc

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: 1st time been there to join SG Digital course.  It's has good environment facing a pond n a swimming pool.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Nice environment outside with fish pond, but 1 of the worst inside. Too many swimming schools using the lanes and left with 1/3 of the pool for the public. Everything in SG runs like a business. The kid's pool is really small. Overall, the swimming pools is not suitable for exercise.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Damn U guys better get Ur info correct. So many directions given, no officers that have good knowledge, I'm going around in circles. Why can't U guys just say if it's for appeals to join the queue rather than making a call that waste my time and energy. Employ officers that have more knowledge and information! I wish I can give 0 stars if I could.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Beautiful community club next to a pond. Comes with many features like dance studios, badminton court, swimming pool, barber, food centre, etc

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.
